# UC-17.1.49 · Cisco ISE Patch and Upgrade Health — Pre/Post-Upgrade Validation

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Operations, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When ISE installs a software update or patch, we watch closely to make sure it succeeds and that user logins keep working afterwards. If the update fails or breaks something, we ring the bell immediately.*

---

## Description

Detects ISE patch rollbacks, failed upgrades, and degraded post-upgrade health (auth-success rate drop or replication lag spike) within 24h after an upgrade event.

## Value

ISE upgrades are months-long projects across many nodes; a single failed step can leave the deployment in a degraded state. Detecting the failure within minutes shortens the recovery window. The post-upgrade health-check arm of this UC catches subtle regressions — a new ISE release breaking an obscure auth flow that only shows up in production.

## Implementation

Confirm logging categories above are at INFO. Deploy the upgrade-status poller (only active during upgrade windows). Schedule the search every 5 min during upgrade windows; daily otherwise.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

On a lab ISE, install a patch that intentionally rolls back. ISE emits 51021. UC-17.1.49 fires.

### Negative scenario

Successful patch emits 51020 only. UC-17.1.49 does not fire.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Logging categories at INFO.
- Upgrade-status poller for windows.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate event flow.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Patch & Upgrade Lifecycle":
- Patch/upgrade history table.
- Pre/post auth-success comparison.
- Top-N upgrade-duration outliers.

Incident response: high-urgency PagerDuty on 51024 / 51021.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **Upgrade stuck** — `application configure ise > 13` and inspect upgrade logs.
- **Patch rollback recurring** — Patch incompatible with existing customisation; engage TAC.
- **Post-upgrade auth drop** — Specific identity-store integration broken by the upgrade; review release notes.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (51020, 51021, 51022, 51023, 51024))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:upgrade_status")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event by node, MessageCode
| eval status = case(MessageCode=="51023", "upgrade_success", MessageCode=="51024", "upgrade_failed", MessageCode=="51020", "patch_success", MessageCode=="51021", "patch_rollback", MessageCode=="51022", "upgrade_started")
| where status IN ("upgrade_failed", "patch_rollback")
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: pending/in-flight upgrades. (2) Pre/post comparison panel: auth-success rate trend over 7 days bracketing the upgrade. (3) Top-N: nodes with longest upgrade duration.

## Known False Positives

- **Patch rollback during testing** — Lab patch rollback fires; tag and exclude.
- **In-progress upgrade** — In a rolling upgrade, individual nodes report 'failed' transiently while waiting for the cluster to converge; only alert on terminal-state failures (51024).

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 Upgrade Guide](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/upgrade_guide/b_ise_upgrade_guide_34.html)
- [Cisco ISE Patches](https://software.cisco.com/download/home/283801620)
